articles.title: AI agents are fast, loose and out of control, MIT study finds - ZDNET
articles.content: The vast majority of agentic AI systems disclose nothing about what safety testing, if any, has been conducted, and many systems have no documented way to shut down a rogue bot, a study by MIT and collaborators found. The results make clear that agentic AI is something of a security nightmare at the moment, a discipline marked by lack of disclosure, lack of transparency, and a striking lack of basic protocols about how agents should operate. articles.content: ## The agreement could help Meta ease its reliance on Nvidia for AI chips, while also aligning the two companies’ goals.
